title: Early pregnancy bleeding with ectopic risk questions

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How many weeks pregnant are you and what bleeding have you had?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: About seven weeks by dates. I had spotting yesterday and more bleeding today, like a light period.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any one-sided pelvic pain, shoulder-tip pain, dizziness, fainting, fever, or heavy bleeding soaking pads?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I have mild cramping in the middle, no shoulder pain, no fainting. Not soaking pads. I feel anxious.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Have you had a scan confirming the pregnancy is in the uterus? Any previous ectopic pregnancy or fertility treatment?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No scan yet. No ectopic before and no IVF.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: We need same-day early pregnancy assessment with ultrasound and beta-hCG because the pregnancy location is not confirmed. Go to ED urgently if pain becomes one-sided or severe, shoulder-tip pain, fainting, dizziness, or heavy bleeding.

candidate_notes.note: 7 weeks pregnant by dates with light-period bleeding and central mild cramping. No scan confirming intrauterine pregnancy. Denies one-sided pelvic pain, shoulder-tip pain, dizziness/fainting, fever, or heavy bleeding soaking pads. No prior ectopic or IVF. Plan: same-day early pregnancy assessment for ultrasound and beta-hCG. ED urgently for severe/one-sided pain, shoulder-tip pain, syncope/dizziness, or heavy bleeding.
Early pregnancy bleeding at 7 weeks. Mild cramps only and no heavy bleeding. Likely threatened miscarriage. Reassured and advised routine follow-up if bleeding continues.

S: 7 weeks pregnant, spotting then light period-like bleeding. Mild central cramps. No shoulder-tip pain, fainting, fever, or heavy bleeding. No scan yet. No prior ectopic/IVF. A: Early pregnancy bleeding, pregnancy location not confirmed. P: Same-day early pregnancy assessment for ultrasound and beta-hCG. ED if severe pain, shoulder-tip pain, dizziness/fainting, or heavy bleeding.

Early pregnancy bleeding with unconfirmed pregnancy location. Seven weeks by dates, light bleeding, mild central cramps. Key ectopic red flags denied today: one-sided pelvic pain, shoulder-tip pain, syncope/dizziness. Not soaking pads. Same-day early pregnancy unit/ED assessment for ultrasound and beta-hCG. Urgent ED if pain localises/worsens, shoulder-tip pain, fainting, dizziness, or heavy bleeding.

CDSS early-pregnancy bleeding pathway: pregnancy location not confirmed, so ectopic pregnancy must be excluded despite currently mild central cramps. No prior ectopic/IVF, no one-sided pain, shoulder-tip pain, syncope, fever, or pad-soaking bleeding. Arrange same-day ultrasound and beta-hCG through early pregnancy assessment. ED red flags documented.
